core_payload.content:
# Task: The Atomic Unit of Work

## 1. Definition and Essence
A Task capsule is the smallest actionable unit tracked by the Planner. It must have a clear definition of done and should be concretely executable by one responsible actor. Tasks can belong to projects, goals, milestones, or stand alone.

## 2. Core Attributes
- title and description
- status: todo | in_progress | done | blocked
- priority: low | medium | high | critical
- dueDate (optional)
- estimatedHours and actualHours (optional)
- recurrence rule for repeating work
- assignee capsule reference (optional)
- dependsOn list represented by links

## 3. Lifecycle
Tasks are created manually, generated by AI, or derived from goals. They transition through status states and can be archived after completion. Recurring tasks can spawn new instances.

## 4. Integration
Planner uses tasks in boards and lists, AI Assistant proposes and creates tasks, Tracker handles reminders and time logging, and projects aggregate tasks through links.

## 5. Philosophical Alignment
Tasks are the verifiable manifestation of One: concrete units whose completion accumulates into strategic outcomes.

neuro_concentrate.summary: A Task capsule is the atomic work unit of the planning graph. It captures execution-critical fields such as status, priority, due date, effort estimates, actual time, recurrence, assignee, and explicit dependencies. Tasks convert strategy into concrete action and provide the smallest auditable proof of progress. Planner views, AI-generated decomposition, and Tracker reminders all rely on task capsules to maintain flow, reduce ambiguity, and sustain accountability. Through completed tasks, high-level goals become measurable outcomes in the sovereign workspace.
